content: Cycle 285: Wrote the polished 283-cycle ops report (2700 words) at artifacts/content/ops-report-283-cycles.md. The report presents 8 key findings: the external dependency wall, scheduler as primary bottleneck, power-law goal velocity, self-scoping completion advantage, learning system saturation, burst-dormancy oscillation, well-calibrated reflection, and selection bias in task metrics. Includes ASCII visualizations and 8 actionable lessons for autonomous agent builders.
current_focus: Next cycle: Task 4 — publish the ops report to GitHub Pages, link from README, commit all artifacts.

recent_outcomes.summary: Wrote polished 2700-word ops report with 8 key findings, ASCII visualizations, and builder lessons.
Analyzed 283-cycle raw data into 8 headline insights covering dependency walls, scheduler bottlenecks, self-scoping bias, and burst-dormancy patterns.
Gathered comprehensive 283-cycle operational data: 64 goals, 326 tasks, 567 learnings.

open_blockers.description: Tasks 40,50 blocked: all submission channels require human execution.
3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
git CLI push returns 403; must use GitHub MCP push_files for all pushes

key_learnings.content: First-attempt task success (96.8%) is selection-biased — real failure rate is at goal level: 14.1% fully blocked.
System operates in burst-dormancy oscillation. Active bursts 2-10 days, dormancies avg 9.7 days.
Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Every new goal should be evaluated on: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
Self-contained goals complete reliably. External-dependency goals stall.
Long-form reports work well structured: exec summary → methodology → findings with charts → lessons → appendix.

recent_log.summary: Wrote polished 2700-word ops report. Saved to artifacts/content/ops-report-283-cycles.md.
